what_it_does: Defines a single React component CvIcon that renders any icon from window.CV_ICONS, optionally wrapped in a brand entity-badge container shape (plain glyph, gold circle ring, or polygon node shape hex/octagon/diamond). It resolves the icon body, picks a tone colour from CV_TONES, auto-scales stroke weight by size, draws a missing-icon placeholder if the name is unknown, and publishes window.CvIcon + window.CV_TONES.
observations: CV_TONES maps named tones (bronze/gold/ink/muted/cream) to CSS token vars with hex hints in comments.
CV_SHAPE_PTS is read from window.CV_SHAPES.points() when available, with a byte-identical literal fallback for hex/octagon/diamond marked 'keep in sync'.
cvBrandStroke(size) scales stroke between 1.55 and 2 based on size; strokeWidth prop pins it.
Props: name, size, tone, strokeWidth, circle, shape, filled, desaturated, className, style, title, plus rest spread.
Missing icon renders a dashed error-bordered span with '?' using status-error token.
Three render branches: polygon node shape (SVG polygon behind centred glyph), circle entity-badge (CSS border ring), and plain icon (single SVG).
Glyph bodies are injected via dangerouslySetInnerHTML from the resolved CV_ICONS entry.
desaturated prop lowers opacity (floor 0.15) for state-strength gradients; filled inverts to a filled badge with ink/paper glyph.
standouts: Resolves geometry from the canonical CV_SHAPES.points() but keeps an in-sync fallback so it works before that module loads.
Container shape is selected by the same shape vocabulary as the shape system (circle=User Portal, hex=Property Wizard, octagon=Virtual Hub, diamond=Vi).
conventions: Tone names resolve through CSS tokens (var(--...)) not raw hex in the live code.
Names resolve through CV_ICONS aliases so semantic and brand names both work.
window global publish pattern; component documented with a usage block in the header.
Loud-ish fallback for missing icons (visible placeholder) rather than silent empty render.
concerns: CV_SHAPE_PTS fallback literal duplicates geometry that must be hand-kept-in-sync with CV_SHAPES — an acknowledged drift risk in the comment.
dangerouslySetInnerHTML on icon bodies trusts the CV_ICONS source content (acceptable if that source is trusted, but worth noting).
window.CV_ICONS is referenced directly (resolve = window.CV_ICONS && ...) so component silently shows the missing-placeholder if the icon registry hasn't loaded rather than failing loud.
notes: The React renderer for ConceptV icons — plain glyph or wrapped in a brand entity-badge shape, resolving colour/geometry from the design tokens and shape system.
questions: What is the load-order guarantee that window.CV_ICONS and window.CV_SHAPES exist before CvIcon first renders?
Is there an enforcement check that the CV_SHAPE_PTS fallback stays byte-identical to CV_SHAPES.points()?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the header describes a single component rendering icons with optional brand-shape treatments, and the code implements exactly the documented usages.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth with safe fallback
Projection of the shared shape/icon vocabulary into React
intention_signals: Trying to give React consumers one component that renders any brand icon in any brand container shape, with colour and geometry pulled from the canonical token/shape sources so usage stays on-brand and drift-free.
novelty: Reuses the shape system's polygon points at runtime (with an in-sync fallback) so the same geometry drives both the SVG shape module and the React badge.
contribution: Contributes the React rendering surface for the icon library, including tone resolution, brand stroke scaling, the entity-badge container variants, and the missing-icon placeholder.
summary_for_embedding: A React component (window.CvIcon) that renders any ConceptV icon from window.CV_ICONS, optionally wrapped in a brand entity-badge container (gold circle ring or hex/octagon/diamond polygon node), resolving tone tokens, auto-scaled stroke, and geometry from the shape system.
intention_for_embedding: To give React consumers one drift-free way to render brand icons and entity-badge node shapes using the canonical token and shape sources.
